revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:五流尊瀧院]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Goryu Sonryuin 07.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.536694|N|133.818861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Temple | Name = Goryū Sonryūin | image = [[File:Goryu Sonryuin 07.jpg|260px]]
Main Hall | Location = Hayashi 952, Kurashiki City, Okayama Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|32|12.1|N|133|49|7.9|E|scale:20000_region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Sect = [[Shugendō]] | Status = Head Temple | Deity = {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|qid=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} | Founded = Traditionally Daibō 1 (701) | Founder = Traditionally a disciple of {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|qid=Q198946|lt=En no Ozunu|1=En no Gyōja}} | Cultural Properties = Portrait Pagoda of Emperor Go-Toba (National Important Cultural Property)
Three-Story Pagoda, Bonshō, Eleven-Faced Kannon Standing Statue, and Tantō (Prefectural Important Cultural Properties) }} [[File:Goryu Sonryuin 13.jpg|200px|thumb|Portrait Pagoda of Emperor Go-Toba
(National Important Cultural Property)]] '''Goryū Sonryūin''' (五流尊瀧院) is a {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|qid=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}} temple located in Hayashi, {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. The temple enshrines the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|qid=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} as its principal deity and serves as the head temple of the Shugendō sect. The abbot is {{ill|Jun Miyake|ja|宮家準|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jun Miyake|1=Hitoshi Miyake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11454056}}, an emeritus professor of Keio University. == Overview == The temple traces its origins to {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|qid=Q198946|lt=En no Ozunu|1=En no Gyōja}}, the legendary founder of Shugendō, who was exiled to Izu Island in 699 (Bunmu 3) after being accused by the imperial court. According to tradition, five of his disciples—Gigaku, Gigen, Gishin, Jugen, and Hōgen—carried the sacred objects of {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|qid=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha|1=Kumano Hongū Taisha}} and established the temple at its current location in 701 (Daibō 1) following a divine revelation. They also founded five related temples, with Sonryūin serving as the central temple. During the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, Goryū Sonryūin received imperial patronage, including donations of land by Emperor Shōmu in 740 (Tenpyō 12). The complex grew to include three shrines collectively known as "New Kumano Sanzan." The temple flourished as a religious center blending {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} (the syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism), though it later declined during the mid-Heian period. Emperor Go-Toba's son, Prince Kakunin, and his fourth son, Prince Yorimasa, revitalized the temple in the early Kamakura period after being exiled to the area. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * '''Stone Portrait Pagoda of Emperor Go-Toba''' Built in 1240 (Ninji 1) to commemorate the first anniversary of Emperor Go-Toba's death, this pagoda is said to house part of the emperor's remains. The pagoda, located south of Kumano Shrine, was designated a National Important Cultural Property in 1956 (Shōwa 31). === Okayama Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties === * '''Three-Story Pagoda''' Constructed in 1820 (Bunsei 3), this pagoda stands 21.5 meters tall and is located adjacent to Kumano Shrine. Designated in 1974 (Shōwa 49). * '''Bonshō (Temple Bell)''' Cast in 1457 (Kōshō 3) by Shakugenjū, this bell measures 129.4 cm in height and 70 cm in diameter. * '''Wooden Standing Statue of Eleven-Faced Kannon''' The temple’s principal deity, created during the Kamakura period. Designated in 1956. * '''Tantō (Short Sword)''' Crafted in 1357 (Enbun 2) by the Yamashiro swordsmith Nobukuni. Designated in 1961 (Shōwa 36). == Access == * {{ill|Seto-Ōhashi Line|en|Seto-Ōhashi Line|qid=Q7456750|lt=JR Seto-Ōhashi Line|1=Seto-Ōhashi Line}}: 25-minute walk southwest from {{ill|Uematsu Station|en|Uematsu Station|qid=Q7877451|lt=Uematsu Station|1=Uematsu Station}} or 20-minute walk north from {{ill|Kimi Station|en|Kimi Station|qid=Q6410088|lt=Kimi Station|1=Kimi Station}}. * {{ill|Seto-Chūō Expressway|en|Seto-Chūō Expressway|qid=Q11566530|lt=Seto-Chūō Expressway|1=Seto-Chūō Expressway}}: 5 minutes east from {{ill|Mizushima Interchange|ja|水島インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizushima Interchange|1=Mizushima interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11129865}}. == References == * Okayama High School Education Research Association Social Studies Division/History Subdivision (ed.), ''New Edition: Historical Walks of Okayama Prefecture,'' Yamakawa Shuppansha, 1991, pp. 102–103. * On-Site Explanatory Panels == See Also == * {{ill|Zenki and Goki|ja|前鬼・後鬼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenki and Goki|1=Zenki and Goki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11397864}} {{translated page|ja|五流尊瀧院|version=102257266|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:五流尊瀧院]] {{Commons category|Goryu-sonryuin}} {{wikidata link|Q11372540}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kurashiki]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties Designated by Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Kurashiki]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Tiantai]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
